Document:
# Aranchind
[bold]Arachnids[/bold] are an [bold][color=#4df]Oxygen-breathing[/color][/bold], [bold][color=#4df]Copper-blooded[/color][/bold] species; sharing traits with insapient spiders found across space.
Arachnids take more [bold][color=#4df]Asphyxiation[/color] damage[/bold] when suffocating, and recover from it slower.
Arachnids have [bold]two extra pockets[/bold] made of web, these [bold]work without a jumpsuit[/bold] but can be [bold]examined by anyone[/bold] at a glance.
Arachnids can [bold]produce silk[/bold] at the cost of hunger, which they can further spin into various structures and implements.
Arachnids have the baseline body temperature of [bold][color=yellow]37°C[/color][/bold] (~[color=yellow]310°K[/color]). They experience the effects of [bold]hypothermia at [color=#4df]-13°C[/color][/bold] and [bold]hyperthermia at [color=#f44]52°C[/color][/bold]. (~[color=#4df]260°K[/color] & [color=#f44]325°K[/color])
Arachnids are [bold]omnivorous[/bold], meaning they can safely digest [bold]meat and plant based foods[/bold], but they can't stomach [bold]raw meat or blood[/bold].
Their bodies can process [bold]2 reagents[/bold] at a time.
They slowly [bold]passively regenerate[/bold] all types of [bold][color=yellow]Brute[/color] damage[/bold], and individually [bold][color=#f44]Heat[/color] damage[/bold].
Arachnid names typically follow a "[bold]Firstname Lastname[/bold]" pattern, superficially resembling taxonomic designations and Latin words.
